static._comment: These fields are set once. Edit manually. Never overwritten by the transformer.

dynamic.install_command: mvn dependency:resolve && pip install -r api/requirements.txt && cd services/scoring-service && npm ci
dynamic.architecture_summary: The project is a polyglot monorepo implementing the bowling scoring kata in four languages. The Java Spring Boot service is the primary API with a REST controller, domain model (PlayerScore), and an in-memory LeaderboardService. A Python FastAPI service, a TypeScript Express microservice, and a Go CLI scorer each independently implement the same bowling scoring algorithm. Docker Compose orchestrates all services along with Redis and Prometheus.

key_components.name: Bowling
key_components.responsibility: Core Java implementation of the bowling game scoring logic with roll() and score() methods.

key_components.name: BowlingApplication
key_components.responsibility: Spring Boot application entry point that bootstraps the Java REST API.

key_components.name: GameController
key_components.responsibility: REST controller exposing /api/score and /api/leaderboard endpoints for bowling game scoring.

key_components.name: LeaderboardService
key_components.responsibility: In-memory thread-safe service that records and ranks player scores on a leaderboard.

key_components.name: PlayerScore
key_components.responsibility: Domain object representing a player's scored bowling game with natural ordering by highest score.

key_components.name: FastAPI main
key_components.responsibility: Python FastAPI service implementing bowling scoring with /health and /score endpoints.

key_components.name: scoring-service index
key_components.responsibility: TypeScript Express microservice exposing bowling scoring via /health and /score HTTP endpoints.

key_components.name: Go bowling game
key_components.responsibility: Go implementation of the bowling scoring logic with Roll() and Score() methods.

key_components.name: Go CLI scorer
key_components.responsibility: Go CLI entry point that accepts roll arguments and prints the calculated bowling score.

key_components.name: ScoringRequest
key_components.responsibility: Request DTO for the POST /api/score endpoint, containing player name and list of rolls.

key_components.name: ScoringResponse
key_components.responsibility: Response DTO for the POST /api/score endpoint, containing player name, score, and rank.

agent_safe_operations: Modify bowling scoring logic in src/main/java/bowling/Bowling.java
Modify bowling scoring logic in api/main.py
Modify bowling scoring logic in pkg/bowling/game.go
Modify bowling scoring logic in services/scoring-service/src/index.ts
Add or modify test cases in src/test/java/bowling/
Add or modify test cases in api/test_scoring.py
Add or modify test cases in pkg/bowling/game_test.go
Add or modify test cases in services/scoring-service/src/scoring.test.ts
Add new API endpoints in GameController.java
Add new test fixtures in api/conftest.py
agent_forbidden_operations: Modify or delete .github/workflows/ CI configuration files
Modify lock files or dependency version pinning in pom.xml, go.mod, package.json, or requirements.txt
Modify or delete .env.example or create .env files with real secrets
Modify agent-context.json, mcp.json, or AGENTS.md (auto-generated agentic context files)
Modify tool template scaffolds in the tools/ directory
Commit real API keys or secrets anywhere in the codebase
Delete or modify the LICENSE file
potential_pitfalls: The project has four independent implementations of bowling scoring (Java, Python, Go, TypeScript) — changes to scoring logic must be synchronized across all four to maintain consistency.
The Java project requires JDK 21 (pom.xml) but CI uses JDK 17 — tests may pass in CI but fail locally if Java version mismatches exist.
Python tests import from 'api.main' which requires the api/ directory to be a package on the Python path; run pytest from the repo root with 'pytest api/' as shown in CI.
The tools/ directory contains template scaffolds with unresolved {{PLACEHOLDER}} variables — these files will not compile and should not be modified by agents.
The Go module path is 'github.com/vb-nattamai/bowling-kata' — all Go imports must use this full module path, not relative imports.
The LeaderboardService is in-memory and not persistent — all scores are lost on restart, which could be surprising when testing via Docker Compose.
The 10th frame in bowling allows up to 3 rolls which is a common source of off-by-one errors; the rolls array is sized to 21 to accommodate this.
The Python conftest.py fixtures use 'api.main' import which depends on running pytest from the repo root, not from within the api/ directory.
The TypeScript scoring service test file imports directly from './index' which also starts the Express server — this may cause port conflicts in test environments.
